sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports difficulty swallowing over the past several weeks, with pain when swallowing larger food pieces. He reports being stressed due to a recent move and drinking more, including pizza and burgers. He also reports some epigastric pain and occasional belly pain. The patient reports weight gain of approximately ten pounds over the past month. He denies blood in the stool.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 130/70 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm. Oxygen saturation is 100%. Temperature is 98.6°F. Neurological exam is unremarkable. Examination of the lungs and heart sounds clear. No rash or lesions are noted. The barium swallow revealed two areas of mild esophageal narrowing.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has acute esophagitis. The symptoms are consistent with esophageal dysfunction.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe protonix 40 mg daily. Prescribe carafate 1 gram four times daily for one month. Schedule an upper endoscopy for follow-up. Advise the patient to avoid citrus fruits, spicy foods, caffeine, and alcohol. Recommend eating slowly and chewing food thoroughly. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week.
